experiments.hypothesis: Establish baseline L2 hit rates for GEMM running in isolation across sizes
experiments.conclusion: L2 hit rates are stable ~78-80% for most sizes. 2K anomaly at 63.1% may be tile/cache alignment issue. GEMM L2 behavior is consistent across sizes.

experiments.hypothesis: L2 hit rate drops significantly when GEMM runs alongside RCCL due to cache evictions
experiments.conclusion: HYPOTHESIS REFUTED. GEMM L2 hit rates are COMPLETELY UNAFFECTED by RCCL overlap (delta < 0.3%). This confirms that MI300X's per-XCD, non-coherent L2 provides isolation between concurrent kernels. NCCL kernels themselves have ~50% L2 hit rate (essentially random). The GEMM performance degradation during overlap is NOT caused by L2 thrashing.
implications: L2 (TCC) is NOT the bottleneck in compute-communication overlap on MI300X
MALL/LLC (coherent, cross-XCD) analysis is critical — needs df-counters
HBM bandwidth contention is a strong candidate — needs HBM_READ/WRITE_BYTES
CU contention and wave quantization remain as other major factors

experiments.hypothesis: Measure actual overlap efficiency for 8K GEMM + all_reduce
experiments.conclusion: 53.1% overlap efficiency. GEMM slowdown 1.13x vs cold-cache baseline (rotating buffers). Comm slowdown 1.62x. The 1.13x GEMM slowdown cannot be explained by L2 thrashing (proven in exp_002). Must be CU contention, MALL/LLC, or HBM bandwidth.
experiments.next_experiment: Need df-counters for MALL_BANDWIDTH_ALL and HBM_READ/WRITE_BYTES to separate MALL/LLC thrashing from HBM bandwidth contention

experiments.hypothesis: Decompose GEMM overlap slowdown into CU contention vs memory bandwidth contention
experiments.conclusion: Work-stealing almost completely eliminates CU contention effects (+1.2%). Memory bandwidth contention is the dominant factor (+7.5%). Network fabric adds +4.3%. This strongly suggests MALL/LLC and HBM counters would show elevated memory traffic during overlap.
experiments.significance: HIGH — cleanly decomposes the 3 contention sources

experiments.hypothesis: Reducing NCCL channels reduces memory bandwidth pressure and improves GEMM overlap performance
experiments.conclusion: HYPOTHESIS REFUTED. GEMM slowdown is CONSTANT at ~1.03x regardless of NCCL channel count (4→32). This means NCCL's memory traffic volume does NOT materially affect GEMM during overlap with work-stealing. The CU contention from NCCL workgroups is the only meaningful factor. Fewer channels improve overlap efficiency simply because comm takes longer (more 'free' overlap time).
experiments.significance: HIGH — proves GEMM with work-stealing is insensitive to NCCL BW load
